payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The guards still keeping track behind you. You find your way to the end of the main road, and it splits into two smaller avenues. You know to take the left that leads deeper into the more political family houses of Syngorn. While your family is not a political house per se, your father, as an ambassador to Emon, was given a home in this location. It is your old home, although it appears to have been even further renovated and is much nicer than you remember, but the door is closed and the dusk sun leads you to pause as you look upon the outside of this abode you have not seen in many, many years.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is exciting for us. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. You guys continue making your way towards the abode. After traversing on the outskirts of what would be the tangleward side of the city, the eastern side, you travel in through an area of buildings that are very beautifully crafted, many of which seem to have stemmed from some sort of a tree growth, but upon closer look you can see that it's actually carved stone to resemble a tree. And then it eventually rises up into a somewhat spherical building, and there are many that build off of that to create this kind of neighborhood of various elven abodes within Syngorn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Fucking hate this place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Me, too. It's hideous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is gorgeous. Look at the craftsmanship. It's incredible.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You make your way towards the end of this particular-- (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Something like that, yes. It's been a little scarce in the Feywilds. More difficult to farm herbs here than you'd think."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, yes, do you have an oolong?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe a chamomile, it would be great.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Let me get her for you, she'll be very like to see both of you, she asks from time to time." She brushes a little lock of hair that's fallen in front of her face behind her ear, and smiles and darts off towards the kitchen. A few moments pass with you guys sitting in awkward silence, looking about the space in the vicinity. Looks to the twins. Eventually Devana comes out and sets tea for all of you across the central table in this meeting area, and behind her you see, clutching the edge of the skirt, you see this tiny, long brown hair that is braided, but the braid's coming undone, tomboyish looking elven girl that peeks around the side of her dress and hides again behind the back. [high-pitched noise]

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's, that's really not necessary in my home. You, sir, your name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Whatever's available would be just fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I think she means your name.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's a curious title."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But you can call him whatever's available, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well, coffee table. What's your name?" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's easier than the actual name.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: For you, it is just Percival, and I am another visiting royal from man's world.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right. You, sir?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I am called the Kingslayer, Chuck Norris, Burt Reynolds. I have many names. You see, when you've traveled a road as long as I have--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Black tea, got it. And you, sir, the large one?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah. Bidet. The name is Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. I'll return with Syldor once he knows you're here. It may be a while if you don't mind waiting, but the tea will come out posthaste."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And Velora? Where's she?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The technical aspects. We're working through it, but we got through it, thank you for your patience. So the last few minutes, we're not quite sure how much you guys may have missed, but the party wiped. We're rolling up new characters. No, for the Monday episode we'll go ahead and make sure to splice in anything that may have been missed for the past minute. But the party had come to the home of Syldor, the father of Vax and Vex. They had a brief discussion with Devana, his new wife. They were given tea after introductions and then briefly discussed the merits of being a warrior with their half-sister Velora Vessar, and upon discussing apparently a war of some kind, Syldor made his way into the room, and you look and you see the long dark hair of Syldor, his pale complexion, his stern face, staring back at you all from within the doorway as he closes it behind him and steps into the room. You see Devana sits up now and comes over to him and says, "My dear, would you like us to leave you alone?" He goes, "Don't worry, it will be fine. Have a seat. Besides, we're all family. Well, some of us, at least." And he looks over to Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And Velora? Where's she?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let me get her for you, she'll be very like to see both of you, she asks from time to time." She brushes a little lock of hair that's fallen in front of her face behind her ear, and smiles and darts off towards the kitchen. A few moments pass with you guys sitting in awkward silence, looking about the space in the vicinity. Looks to the twins. Eventually Devana comes out and sets tea for all of you across the central table in this meeting area, and behind her you see, clutching the edge of the skirt, you see this tiny, long brown hair that is braided, but the braid's coming undone, tomboyish looking elven girl that peeks around the side of her dress and hides again behind the back. [high-pitched noise]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back, sorry about that. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What a battle!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Syldor crosses his fingers together and, "I can give you a letter that will grant you entry to a few of our general storehouses. However, most of you are outsiders and others have been ostracized in their own right."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And I take it you're their leader."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Of the dragons? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He very slowly sighs while grabbing his tea.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've clearly lost your father. It's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: His intelligence is very low, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: In the interest of dealing with Thordak, we are going to need access to libraries, any of your people who may know something about this bog, and accommodations befitting those of us who are titled.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As well as access to certain parts of the city. We need some items to acquire within the city before we set out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Also, it would be nice to find shelter for those of us who are not titled.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And I had asked for an oolong tea. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is more of an assam, I would say? A black tea.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is technically what you do, though, isn't it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Devana says, "I'm terribly sorry, I didn't mean that--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, not you.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She runs off to the kitchen. A few minutes pass, and Devana comes out holding the tea on the cart and goes, "Who taught my daughter to say 'blue sparkly poo?'"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Look, we're never going to be a happy family.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No. That is right."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But if you want to make reparations, help us on our path. It will only help you on yours.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He stands up from his chair and walks his way back to his study and closes the door behind him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, fuck. Guess he's not into us now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I thought we weren't getting in a fight!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, this is good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you want me to take a dump somewhere?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Not yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, headphones, darling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She pulls your hand down, looks at you, says, "Take a dump!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. I mean, shoot!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I like him."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's gnomish. Doesn't mean the same thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's now transfixed on Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Darling, he takes blue sparkly poos!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Blue sparkle poos? Can you teach me?" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have any blueberries?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm going to ask my mom if we've got blueberries."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

payload.raw_text: "Good day." And Syldor bows, you all slowly leave the door, and right before the door closes behind you hear a little voice go, "Shit." (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You see Devana's looking cornered, it's like, (shushing noises). Syldor actually starts laughing. "I hope that's good day, then!"

payload.raw_text: "Noted and noted." And he turns around and leaves the three other guards standing outside of your rooms to do about as you please.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Rolls it back up, hands it back. "I will retrieve your necessary meal, drink, and other requests you may have."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Thank you. I want anything but chicken.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys did wake up about four hours ago?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's "shedule" the meeting.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Or maybe we could send one of the shitty guards to go "shedule" it for us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is he back from getting us food? Oh no, I hope he's not, because I want to send a second one out there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh! Because you're a lady.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Because I'm a baroness.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't think they're going to leave us all alone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have made such a terrible mistake. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You go and check, you check the hallway, and the guard hasn't returned and the three suddenly spin around as the door opens.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hello! We require a meeting with the High Warden.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do a little gust cantrip from behind the door to make her hair blow in the wind. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I need to take this with me. To make the meeting schedulable."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: "Shedulable."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Shedulable."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Bidet!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: When would we like the meeting? Tonight or tomorrow?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tomorrow.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You guys make your way outside into the city streets of Syngorn to hopefully wait for the other guard, who returns about ten minutes later to tell you, "It has been 'sheduled.'"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I like it here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You finish your meal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's delicious!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And prepare yourselves for an excursion into the city of Syngorn to seemingly steal a piece of the threshold crest and the High Warden Tyrelda's travel hat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn into Minxie and resign to the idea that I'm going to be sleeping with Garmelie and Trinket and just cuddle next to them in Minxie mode.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you coming with us, though, Keyleth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we're not sleeping?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. It's like one in the afternoon, so we'll probably just go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But you know, blow a couple more spells.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So that's one use of your beast form.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just come on, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just save it. You can stay like that, just walk close to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Am I going to freak people out if I walk around like this?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: God, I hope so.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck them, that's what I say.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Let me see your papers. Oh, I see they're right here. Very well. Follow me, please." He turns around and walks his way into the building. You all follow suit and are led through the main entryway to the palace itself. On the inside you can see these giant archways that the pillars that hold them up look like trees that spread to the top like the stone boughs actually hold a canopy made of marble with beautiful portraits and long artistic renditions of the creation of Syngorn, the people, each bloodline that has progressed and taken on the central political structure of the city. As it goes, you can see busts of people who have been of historical importance to Syngorn, and eventually you're brought to the very end of this hallway. Amongst a series of other offshoot hallways and doors, there is the next portion of this journey, this large central chamber. The door is already partially ajar. You are led into what seems to be the equivalent of a throne room, but it appears that there is a half-circle table in the center of the room and a series of chairs that now sit empty across from one side. Your side. The other side of the table, you see sitting quietly and patiently, an older elven woman, you can see the grey in her hair and the crow's-nests at the corner of her eyes, which in elven years means she's quite old, she's probably close to 150 or 200 years old, by your gathering. They can live quite a long time. She would be in the equivalent of her late 50s.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: G'day mate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We do. We have a meeting with the High Warden Tyrelda.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Strange. It has been some time since you've been within the city. Welcome."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The guard leaps back for a second. "That's much easier."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just watch closely, buddy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You step back, you all find your seats.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just talking to my boobs. No big deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Buddy, buddy. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This meeting room pavilion, and as you all have your seat, you see the High Warden waiting there patiently, smile on her face but definitely an expression that shows both intelligence and guard. As you all sit, she looks about the group, asks, "So you've all come and made arrangements to meet with me. You've come quite a ways and with very short notice. I'm curious as to what your reasonings are. Please, state your business."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You probably recognize the two of us even if you don't know us. We're Syldor's children. This is my sister Vex'ahlia. I'm Vax'ildan.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well. The Shademirk, as far as I understand, was once a very beautiful landscape. That once was the fountain heart of the Gilded Run that provided the flowing rivers that spun throughout this entire region. There was an archfey many years before who was turned on by his beloved, who had been growing so spiteful, so full of angst and anger and vengeful thoughts that it turned his heart dark and black and he began to spill into the fen around him. It was there that these lands became the Shademirk. It was there that he became one with the land and corrupted it and turned it. Dryads turned foul and shadowed. Other creatures began to crawl up from the bog itself, and to this day feed off the life of those they can reach. The only thing that holds its spread at bay is the Gilded Run, the rapids. Now, it's found balance, and we watched it spread, as those who've lived here for a long time do. It does not force any sort of immediate threat to any of us, and it serves its own purpose. It has its new-- I shouldn't say new, it's been hundreds of years now-- but its own life, its own biome. And we could not be ones to mess with that, if you will. But it is dangerous, and it is, for those who have scouted far enough to see it, say it is akin to wading through an oil slick. It is to walk into a hungry swamp that never gives up its need to feed."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well. You stroll in here with a great deal of arrogance and an air of self-importance. Not in a way that is negative, more in a way that means you aspire, and I hope, not reaching beyond your means. If you have indeed felled these two members of the Conclave as you say, then perhaps this errand to the Shademirk is not as dangerous as one would expect. I would not deny you this, and if you indeed have these capabilities, you should have no issue surviving the Mirk, you and your companions."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Syldor sent us here because he said you would know what we might face in the Shademirk.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well. The Shademirk, as far as I understand, was once a very beautiful landscape. That once was the fountain heart of the Gilded Run that provided the flowing rivers that spun throughout this entire region. There was an archfey many years before who was turned on by his beloved, who had been growing so spiteful, so full of angst and anger and vengeful thoughts that it turned his heart dark and black and he began to spill into the fen around him. It was there that these lands became the Shademirk. It was there that he became one with the land and corrupted it and turned it. Dryads turned foul and shadowed. Other creatures began to crawl up from the bog itself, and to this day feed off the life of those they can reach. The only thing that holds its spread at bay is the Gilded Run, the rapids. Now, it's found balance, and we watched it spread, as those who've lived here for a long time do. It does not force any sort of immediate threat to any of us, and it serves its own purpose. It has its new-- I shouldn't say new, it's been hundreds of years now-- but its own life, its own biome. And we could not be ones to mess with that, if you will. But it is dangerous, and it is, for those who have scouted far enough to see it, say it is akin to wading through an oil slick. It is to walk into a hungry swamp that never gives up its need to feed."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well. You stroll in here with a great deal of arrogance and an air of self-importance. Not in a way that is negative, more in a way that means you aspire, and I hope, not reaching beyond your means. If you have indeed felled these two members of the Conclave as you say, then perhaps this errand to the Shademirk is not as dangerous as one would expect. I would not deny you this, and if you indeed have these capabilities, you should have no issue surviving the Mirk, you and your companions."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Syldor sent us here because he said you would know what we might face in the Shademirk.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "This was, look, I apologize if your friend is clumsy, but this wasn't part of our job description, okay, it's been a very--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perform check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. Can it be acrobatics, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, acrobatics is avoiding the fall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit. Okay. 19?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to take some shit down with me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And you do, delightfully so. You fall over, trip, go into a tumble, grab arm over pieces of metallic armor, (crashing sound).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, oh god, did you see that--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, Vex, are you okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think there was a marble on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no, what? Keyleth turns to the guard. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was Lady Vex'ahlia, the Baroness of Whitestone. I could sue you! I could sue you for negligence.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a sleight-of-hand check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hold on, he's making a check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You take the hat. You place it away. Everyone seems to be focused on the currently yelling Vex'ahlia--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ow, I think I hurt my wrist!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You will get a letter from me, good sir. You will get a letter, okay?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: But it's been there for a while, so like the roots are growing out and everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You had an older tobacco box.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: An old tobacco box. All right. Yeah, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Braum at this point goes, "I hope you've all found something you liked."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, I grab something random, too. I mean, if we're all doing it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You reach into the Syngorean Wal-mart gift bag on the way out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, secret Santa.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Come on. Roll for value.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. I'll do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No whammies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You bring out what looks to be a really intricately carved music box made of dark wood.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that's neat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you shake it, you hear (ting-ting) little remnants of the last winding come through, and it seems to still play.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Mine. I'm writing it down.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right, I want to reach in and grab something out of there too. All right, fine!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You reach in and you pull out a potato. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I don't know what any of that means, but I assume that's good, and I thank you for that. Goodbye."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're down to one!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey gang, I feel like we've lost focus here. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we should--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: --get out of here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, do we find any broken threshold crests?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do not.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, wait, do we find any pictures of threshold crests? Or a postcard?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We leave.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll just go.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We got the hat.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We have a plan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Braum leads you out of the room, closes and locks it behind, you guys walk back to the hallway that split off, you go past the large metal door again, find your way up into the foyer area at the front of the palace, he goes, "Well, I hope I've been of help to you. I hope I've delivered your interest in seeing our fine selection of helmets and hats here in Syngorn?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I am more than satisfied. I will write a five-star review.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You mean since you guys woke up this morning, I'd say six hours?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if you guys noticed, but there was this recessed door up in the queenie's room, but I don't know if maybe she would have some fancy stuff back there. Can't shake these guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, some of us could slip in anyway, but what do you think we will find there?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, I don't know. I just noticed, because I wasn't paying attention to all the talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think Percy and I can fake them out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fake them out? What do you mean, fake them out?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, is he around? Don't say it out loud.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's no fake. It's not faking. I've got a plan. It's not fake, I've got a plan. It's a good plan. But we should sit, recuperate, go somewhere quiet and figure out how we're getting to the bog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long have we been awake at this point?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He snaps his fingers and you feel like this sudden, this almost unnoticeable pressure at the base of your neck that you had felt since you made the deal release and absolve, on both of you. "All right, so. Well, at least I got the hat out of it. So. The Shademirk, then? Come, follow me, grab your things." (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, Keyleth. The thing we talked about.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I start casting Stone Shape right under the threshold of the door to the room that we're in, and I cut out a crest from the threshold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What crest do you create? What does it look like?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A Whitestone crest. From the threshold of the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We break it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's a piece of your threshold crest. (laughing and cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Don't cast Friends on people. It's not nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as his ears, his pointed ears slowly slink back and his hair stands up all across the back of his head, his beard almost bristles out into spines as the hat goes (poink) and pops off and hits the ground. He snatches it up. Grabs the piece.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's your threshold crest. Piece of a threshold crest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yay!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, it appears that our deal has been kept." (laughing and cheering)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys push on further northward at the behest of Garmelie, you push for another two hours or so through the Ever-Shifting Grasses, and as you get further and further north, the color of the grass seems to go from that deep verdant green to almost a maroon purplish color.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But I'm loosey! I'm a loosey goose.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That would look amazing on an epitaph, I'm just saying.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We can mist there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Would that save time, I don't remember how fast we go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I might have to take a hot nap.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A hot nap?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Didn't you guys just take a hot nap? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. The answer is yes. I can do all those things.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, let's travel for a bit and then we'll take a proper break.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If we can get to the edge of the bog, then we'll take a sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, I think the edge of the bog is kind of far, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll start our journey, and the next leg of our journey.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's get going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll go until we're tired.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I like the sound of that."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. (belches) (laughing and cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Most impressive for a lady."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. That's a baroness burp.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Robert Durst shit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So. About another half an hour of travel before in the distance ahead of you can see the dark edge of what looks to be another forest ahead of you, wide and almost end-to-end, you see it. A lot of these wooded areas almost meet the horizon, it's like you're stepping into just an endless wall of trees. As you begin to approach closer to the edge, Garmelie looks up and says, "All right. So we have choices now. We have the Moonbrush before us, or we have the Green Sea, which is far seaward of here. I would recommend the Moonbrush."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do an interpretive dance.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, persuade that plant.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was leaning.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That was a leaner.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That jumped, but that's still fine, okay, it's. Persuasion check? Oh yeah, that's not bad. 24.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What was it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Eight plus six plus seven. (counting) 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's more than that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Between the interpretive dance of Keyleth amongst the tattered angry grasses, the beautiful vocal stylings of Scanlan Shorthalt, and Percy speaking out a wondrous poem towards the Ever-Shifting Grasses, you watch as the deep bright red subsides into the relaxed purple, and eventually goes back to a dull green, and you find your traveling no longer impeded as it once was.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Good job!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good job!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You successfully parlayed with a field.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: First doors, then grass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't talk shit to grass!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How do you want to mow this? (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You are... you've been awake for about 12 hours now, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Then we should go the other way. Because we don't trust a fucking thing you say.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, the High Warden did say--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: -- to avoid the Green Sea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why do you recommend the Moonbrush?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Because it's the only real route to get to the Gilded Run."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What is the Gilded Run?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Gilded Run are the rivers that surround it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right, those.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes. You're looking for the Shademirk. You have to get through the Gilded Run to get the Shademirk. You have to go through the Moonbrush to get to the Gilded Run."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where's the theater?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No theater!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, so we're going through the brushy moon?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes! Come, come."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah? Are you okay with that, Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll allow it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long have we been awake?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Certainly. About another 40 minutes of foot travel before you begin to hit the edge of the Moonbrush Forest. As you approach, Garmelie says, "All right, so things you should know about the Moonbrush. One: it is home to a number of denizens." And as you look overhead, you can see it's a thick fog that permeates the entirety of this forest. You can see the outline of every tree trunk towards the outer rim of this entire brush. The canopy of the trees themselves are like long weeping willows that sag and give this very ominous feeling to the entire forest. You can see small mountain spires breaking through portions of the tree line in the distance occasionally, miles between each other, sometimes a cluster far closer together. He says, "There is a clan of sorts that lives amongst the forest, we'll try our best to avoid. They are natural hunters and they are of the lycan persuasion."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, the High Warden did say--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: -- to avoid the Green Sea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why do you recommend the Moonbrush?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Because it's the only real route to get to the Gilded Run."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What is the Gilded Run?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Gilded Run are the rivers that surround it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right, those.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes. You're looking for the Shademirk. You have to get through the Gilded Run to get the Shademirk. You have to go through the Moonbrush to get to the Gilded Run."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where's the theater?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No theater!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, so we're going through the brushy moon?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes! Come, come."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah? Are you okay with that, Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll allow it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long have we been awake?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are... you've been awake for about 12 hours now, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If we want to mist there, then we have to sleep. But we'll press on for a few more hours.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 28. This bone belongs to a larger bird, like a great eagle, you can't quite specifically make out what it is, but it's been carved with small letters into it of a language you can't understand. It looks like it's some sort of a ritual item that's been placed there in the ground. But as you pick up and make this moment noticed--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do I see any kind of markings on the obelisks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Get out of here!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What'd you get?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They just look rough and old. They've been placed there. It's old magic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: God, you're so bad at D&D!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I suck!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you do see around the ground right out in front of this cave what appears to be hundreds and hundreds of small objects, piles, what looks like fruits and flowers and bones and stone carvings, all sorts of things placed in front of this cave.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I do a nature check on one of the bones to see if I can identify where it came from?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So you head out and take one of the bones and look at it. Go ahead and make a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the closest bone, I don't want to, ooh. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That ain't nothing to sneeze at.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's not. 28.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So. I've got to finish this quickly because we've got to get to the Q and A, but as of this point in time, and I'll give you an ability card, but Trinket now benefits from your Hunter's Mark. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As he's looking at you, you look down and you can see the necklace that you hold is actually shimmering with a blue light that passed through you, and it slowly fades to its dark normal form.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're part bear now!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. That's your new item card for Raven's Slumber. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: AUDIENCE MEMBER
preceding_turns.text: What's it do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And has an ability called Primal Fury you can use once per short rest that I'll get to next episode. As you all gather around this moment of silence, this quiet tension that has now dispersed, you see Trinket lift up Vex from the base of her cloak and put her back on her feet, nuzzles the side of her, and you can see the trees overhead open up a little wider than they were before, leaving some moonlight to hit the two of them. And that's where we'll go ahead and leave this session this week. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As he's looking at you, you look down and you can see the necklace that you hold is actually shimmering with a blue light that passed through you, and it slowly fades to its dark normal form.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're part bear now!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. That's your new item card for Raven's Slumber. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: AUDIENCE MEMBER
preceding_turns.text: What's it do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. I've got to finish this quickly because we've got to get to the Q and A, but as of this point in time, and I'll give you an ability card, but Trinket now benefits from your Hunter's Mark. (cheering)
